_description: react-jsonschema-form (RJSF) — 100% MIT. Two separate objects required: 'schema' (JSON Schema Draft-07) and 'uiSchema' (RJSF rendering hints). No drag-and-drop builder. Conditional show/hide requires a custom ObjectFieldTemplate or rjsf-conditionals package.

witness_signature.title: Witness Signature
witness_signature.description: Requires a custom react-signature-canvas widget — no built-in signaturepad in RJSF

then._note: RJSF honours required changes from if/then/else but does NOT hide fields — fields remain visible regardless. Use a custom ObjectFieldTemplate or @rjsf-team/rjsf-conditionals for show/hide.

witness_signature.ui:help: Custom widget — register via widgets prop: <Form widgets={{ signaturePad: SignaturePadWidget }} />

_rendering_example.code: import Form from '@rjsf/core'; import validator from '@rjsf/validator-ajv8'; <Form schema={schema} uiSchema={uiSchema} validator={validator} onSubmit={({ formData }) => handleSubmit(formData)} />

pros: 100% MIT — no commercial licence needed for any feature
JSON Schema standard — schemas reusable with other validators (AJV, Zod)
Direct TypeScript types for the schema
cons: No visual drag-and-drop builder — developer-only workflow
Conditional show/hide requires custom code; not declarative
No built-in signature pad — custom widget required
Non-developer admins cannot configure templates without custom builder UI
